event_types.description: A parent request entered the Ghost runtime.
Conversation owner, provider, and parent strategy were resolved.
A delegated work item and its orchestration linkage were created.
Delegated execution was blocked or unavailable before worker execution.
A direct or delegated runtime task ledger was started.
A runtime task ledger completed with success or failure.
An approval item was created or surfaced for operator action.
An approval item reached a terminal governance state.
A resolved approval produced an explicit governed outcome such as allow, deny, or cancellation.
A workflow or durable artifact was published or promoted.
The canonical user-visible outcome for a request was persisted.

relationships.description: Every request should yield one owner decision context.
Technical-work routing may create a delegation.
Direct routing starts a parent runtime task.
Executable delegated work starts a delegated runtime task.
Blocked delegated work may create an approval record.
Runtime work may emit a durable artifact.
Runtime completion yields the persisted outcome.
